Top 12 Most Popular Cat Breeds That Every Feline Lover Should Know
Explore unique feline breeds, traits, and their global popularity
The Spruce / Phoebe Cheong
Key Takeaways
**Popularity and Characteristics:** The article highlights the distinctive characteristics and origins of the 12 most popular cat breeds, such as the Siamese, known for their intelligence and vocal skills, and the large and gentle Maine Coon.
**Lifespan and Care:** It underscores the importance of indoor lifestyles for cats to ensure longevity and the need for grooming in breeds like Persians.
**Pet Ownership Insights:** The piece provides insights on cat ownership, including which cat breeds are more suitable for families and those that might require extra care or attention.
These 12 cat breeds are the most popular worldwide. Whether due to their unique physical traits, appearances in movies, or affectionate personalities, they have left a lasting, positive impression on cat lovers globally.
A cat is either domestic, purebred, or hybrid. Domestic cats lack a pedigree lineage, while purebreds have one. Hybrids result from crossbreeding domesticated cats with wild feline varieties. The United States-based Cat Fanciers' Association recognizes 45 distinct purebred cat breeds.
Read on to find out the 12 popular cat breeds around the world.
Indoor cats have the longest lifespans, living up to 20 years. Outdoor cats rarely make it past 10 years. Outdoor cats are prone to infections from other animals, can get into fights, may be attacked by predators, or may get run over by vehicles. Outdoor cats may also maim or kill outdoor birds and other wildlife.
How 5 Popular Cat Breeds Got Their Names
01
of 12


 Siamese 
 


 
 Credit: Jovana Rikalo / Stocksy United

 Popular since the 19th century, this cat breed originated in Thailand (formerly known as Siam). The Siamese has been a foundational breed for the Oriental shorthair, sphynx, and Himalayan. Most Siamese cats have distinct markings called "points," which are the areas of coloration on their face, ears, feet, and tail. Two varieties of Siamese cats have an "apple" shaped head and chubbier body and the other features a larger head and slender body. The animated Disney film "Lady and the Tramp" featured Siamese cats singing a song, demonstrating their intelligence and vocal skills. Many Siamese cats that originated from Thailand had a kink in their tail—a trait bred out of most Siamese, but you can still see it in street cats in Thailand.

Breed Overview
Height: Up to 14 inchesWeight: 5 to 8 poundsCoat and Color: Short coat in seal, chocolate, blue, and lilac with blue eyesLife Expectancy: 12 to 15 years
Jovana Rikalo / Stocksy United

Most Siamese cats have distinct markings called "points," which are the areas of coloration on their face, ears, feet, and tail. Two varieties of Siamese cats have an "apple" shaped head and chubbier body and the other features a larger head and slender body. The animated Disney film "Lady and the Tramp" featured Siamese cats singing a song, demonstrating their intelligence and vocal skills. Many Siamese cats that originated from Thailand had a kink in their tail—a trait bred out of most Siamese, but you can still see it in street cats in Thailand.
Breed Overview
Height: Up to 14 inches
Weight: 5 to 8 pounds
Coat and Color: Short coat in seal, chocolate, blue, and lilac with blue eyes
Life Expectancy: 12 to 15 years
02
of 12


 Persian 
 



 
 Credit: Cara Dolan / Stocksy United

 Also lovingly referred to as one of the "smushed-face" cats, the Iranian cat, or Shiraz cat (named for a city in Iran), Persians have beautiful, long fur coats. They can come in almost any color and have a flat face when compared to most other breeds of cats. Persians are featured in many movies, artworks, and commercials and have been one of the most popular breeds of cats for decades. Their coats demand regular grooming, otherwise, matting will result. Like many purebreds, they are prone to a variety of diseases, including renal and cardiac problems.

Breed Overview
Height: 14 to 18 inchesWeight: 7 to 12 poundsCoat and Color: Long coat in solid (white, black, cream), tabby, calico, bi-color, silver and gold, shaded, and smokeLife Expectancy: 15 to 20 years
Cara Dolan / Stocksy United
Also lovingly referred to as one of the "smushed-face" cats, the Iranian cat, or Shiraz cat (named for a city in Iran), Persians have beautiful, long fur coats. Breed Overview
Height: 14 to 18 inches
Weight: 7 to 12 pounds
Coat and Color: Long coat in solid (white, black, cream), tabby, calico, bi-color, silver and gold, shaded, and smoke
Life Expectancy: 15 to 20 years
03
of 12


 Maine Coon 
 



 
 Credit: Jorbasa Fotografie / Flickr / CC By 2.0

 Known for its large stature and thick fur coat, the Maine coon is a cat that is difficult to ignore. Hailing from the state of Maine and the state’s official cat, the Maine coon is a gentle giant. They are great hunters and were popularized after the CFA recognized them as purebred in the late 1970s. They remain one of the most popular cat breeds. Many Maine coons have polydactylism, or extra toes, which makes their already large feet even larger. This great feature helps for hunting in the snow since large feet act as snowshoes. The classic Maine coon coloration is a brown tabby, but this breed can come in almost any color.

Breed Overview
Height: Up to 16 inchesWeight: 8 to 20 poundsCoat and Color: Long, double coat and green, gold, or copper eyesLife Expectancy: 12 to 15 years
Jorbasa Fotografie / Flickr / CC By 2.0

Height: Up to 16 inches
Weight: 8 to 20 pounds
Coat and Color: Long, double coat and green, gold, or copper eyes
Life Expectancy: 12 to 15 years
04
of 12


 Ragdoll 
 



 
 Credit: Peter Munks / Flickr / CC BY 2.0

 Ragdoll cats get their name from their docile temperament. They tend to go limp when picked up, much like a rag doll. At one time, people thought they couldn’t feel pain, but that is not true. Ragdolls look a lot like long-haired Siamese cats with pointed color patterns. They also have distinctive blue eyes and dog-like personalities, following their owners around the house.

Breed Overview
Height: 11 to 13 inchesWeight: 15 to 20 poundsCoat and Color: Medium-length and silky with many different colors and patterns with blue eyesLife Expectancy: 15 to 25 years
Continue to 5 of 12 below
Peter Munks / Flickr / CC BY 2.0

Ragdolls look a lot like long-haired Siamese cats with pointed color patterns. They also have distinctive blue eyes and dog-like personalities, following their owners around the house.
Breed Overview
Height: 11 to 13 inches
Weight: 15 to 20 pounds
Coat and Color: Medium-length and silky with many different colors and patterns with blue eyes
Life Expectancy: 15 to 25 years
05
of 12


 Bengal 
 



 
 Credit: Sean McGrath / Flickr / CC BY 2.0

 Bengals are wild-looking cats—literally. Their markings make them look more like they belong in the jungle rather than in your home, but they are domesticated. They are talkative and require a lot of exercise. They come in many colors with patterns like spots and rosettes. Their name comes from the Asian leopard cat’s taxonomic name, Prionailurus bengalensis. Bengals were initially bred from domestic cats and a wildcat. They also get their beautiful patterns from this wildcat.

Breed Overview
Height: 13 to 16 inchesWeight: 6 to 12 poundsCoat and Color: Shorthaired, spotted, or marbled coat with patterns in black, chocolate, or gray/silver with green or gold eyesLife Expectancy: 12 to 15 years
Sean McGrath / Flickr / CC BY 2.0

06
of 12


 Abyssinian 
 



 
 Credit: Josef Timar / Getty Images

 Originally from Ethiopia (previously known as Abyssinia), the Abyssinian cat has a distinctive ruddy agouti coat that gives the breed its unique banded look. It has tall, pointy ears, a wedge-shaped head, and a slender and muscular body. Personality-wise, Abys are active, curious cats that frequently follow owners around. Their playful, dog-like demeanor endears them to their humans.

Breed Overview
Height: 8 to 10 inchesWeight: 8 to 10 poundsCoat and Color: Short-haired red/orange agouti fur coat with bands of light and dark coloring; other colors can include cinnamon, fawn, and blueLife Expectancy: 12 to 15 years
Josef Timar / Getty Images

It has tall, pointy ears, a wedge-shaped head, and a slender and muscular body. Personality-wise, Abys are active, curious cats that frequently follow owners around. Breed Overview
Height: 8 to 10 inches
Weight: 8 to 10 pounds
Coat and Color: Short-haired red/orange agouti fur coat with bands of light and dark coloring; other colors can include cinnamon, fawn, and blue
Life Expectancy: 12 to 15 years
07
of 12


 Birman 
 



 
 Credit: Giulia Chironna / Getty Images

 Birmans are another color-pointed cat like the Siamese and ragdoll. They have blue eyes and a medium-long coat but no undercoat, the primary trait that sets them apart from Persians and Himalayans. Birmans were the original stock for breeding ragdolls, so they look very similar, but they have slightly different markings and personalities. They are fun, social cats that love attention—from their chosen person. They are more of a one-person cat than others.

Breed Overview
Height: 8 to 10 inchesWeight: 6 to 15 poundsCoat and Color: Long and silky coat in seal, blue, red, chocolate, cream, and tortie colors, including standard or lynx pattern points and deep blue eyesLife Expectancy: 15 to 20 years
Giulia Chironna / Getty Images

08
of 12


 Oriental Shorthair 
 



 
 Credit: TaniaVdB / Pixabay

 At a glance, you may think this slender cat looks like a Siamese, but the Oriental shorthair is a separate breed. Unlike Siamese cats, they usually have green eyes and many color patterns and colors. The Oriental can also have long hair. Oriental shorthair cats are prone to skin cancer and getting cold due to their lack of fur; they are often seen wearing sweaters.

Breed Overview
Height: 9 to 11 inchesWeight: 7 to 12 poundsCoat and Color: Short, smooth coat with many color possibilities: solids, shaded, smoke, and tabby with green, blue, gold, and yellow eyesLife Expectancy: 15 to 20 years
Continue to 9 of 12 below
Oriental Shorthair
TaniaVdB / Pixabay
At a glance, you may think this slender cat looks like a Siamese, but the Oriental shorthair is a separate breed. Unlike Siamese cats, they usually have green eyes and many color patterns and colors. The Oriental can also have long hair. Oriental shorthair cats are prone to skin cancer and getting cold due to their lack of fur; they are often seen wearing sweaters.
Breed Overview
Height: 9 to 11 inches
Weight: 7 to 12 pounds
Coat and Color: Short, smooth coat with many color possibilities: solids, shaded, smoke, and tabby with green, blue, gold, and yellow eyes
Life Expectancy: 15 to 20 years
09
of 12


 Sphynx 
 



 
 Credit: Wunderfool / Getty Images

 If you are looking for a cat that doesn't shed, then the sphynx is for you. Sphynx cats look unique due to their lack of fur and are very social, active, dog-like felines. They look like they belong in the laps of royalty, but they are playful and goofy and act more like court jesters despite their regal appearance.

Breed Overview
Height: 8 to 10 inchesWeight: 8 to 10 poundsCoat and Color: Hairless with white, black, red, chocolate, lavender, tabby, tortoiseshell, calico, pointed, and mink colorsLife Expectancy: 15 to 20 years
Wunderfool / Getty Images

They look like they belong in the laps of royalty, but they are playful and goofy and act more like court jesters despite their regal appearance.
Breed Overview
Height: 8 to 10 inches
Weight: 8 to 10 pounds
Coat and Color: Hairless with white, black, red, chocolate, lavender, tabby, tortoiseshell, calico, pointed, and mink colors
Life Expectancy: 15 to 20 years
10
of 12


 Devon Rex 
 



 
 Devon Rex cats have unique, wavy hair.
Credit: Andrea Abbott / Getty Images

 Devon rex cats have wavy, soft short hair and slender bodies with enormous ears. The Devon Rex is a playful, active cat described as "a monkey in a catsuit." This kitty will steal your heart with its lively, sweet disposition. They are also trainable and adept at learning tricks.

Breed Overview
Height: 10 to 12 inchesWeight: 7 to 12 poundsCoat and Color: Short, fine, wavy coat in solid, tabby, and shaded varietiesLife Expectancy: 15 to 20 years
Andrea Abbott / Getty Images
Devon rex cats have wavy, soft short hair and slender bodies with enormous ears. The Devon Rex is a playful, active cat described as "a monkey in a catsuit." This kitty will steal your heart with its lively, sweet disposition. They are also trainable and adept at learning tricks.
Breed Overview
Height: 10 to 12 inches
Weight: 7 to 12 pounds
Coat and Color: Short, fine, wavy coat in solid, tabby, and shaded varieties
Life Expectancy: 15 to 20 years
11
of 12


 Himalayan 
 



 
 Himalayan cats are color pointed.
Credit: Kryssia Campos / Getty Images

 Like the Persian, the Himalayan cat is considered a sub-breed in some cat associations and a separate breed in others. Himalayans were bred from crossing Persians and Siamese to achieve their color points. They love attention and affection from their owners, have sweet personalities, and are playful.

Breed Overview
Height: 10 to 12 inchesWeight: 9 to 12 poundsCoat and Color:  Dense undercoat with a long overcoat that comes in pointed colors and patterns in gray, blue, chocolate, lilac, and creamLife Expectancy: Up to 18 years
Kryssia Campos / Getty Images
Like the Persian, the Himalayan cat is considered a sub-breed in some cat associations and a separate breed in others. Himalayans were bred from crossing Persians and Siamese to achieve their color points. They love attention and affection from their owners, have sweet personalities, and are playful.
Breed Overview
Height: 10 to 12 inches
Weight: 9 to 12 pounds
Coat and Color:  Dense undercoat with a long overcoat that comes in pointed colors and patterns in gray, blue, chocolate, lilac, and cream
Life Expectancy: Up to 18 years
12
of 12


 American Shorthair 
 



 
 American shorthair cats can come in many patterns and colors.
Credit: Marc Henrie / Getty Images

 An all-American breed, the American shorthair cat is an intelligent, shorthaired feline that has a round face and short ears. They are often confused with domestic mixed-breed cats but are purebred. Its breed name changed from domestic shorthair cats in 1966 to differentiate them. This medium-sized cat has a gentle nature that makes this breed a great companion for families with children or other pets. The American shorthair is one of the most popular pedigreed cat breeds in North America.

Breed Overview
Height: 8 to 10 inchesWeight: 8 to 12 poundsCoat and Color: Shorthaired coat with a wide variety of possibilities, including white, black, cream, blue, brown, chinchilla, tortoiseshell, cameo, and many moreLife Expectancy: 15 to 20 years
American Shorthair
Marc Henrie / Getty Images
An all-American breed, the American shorthair cat is an intelligent, shorthaired feline that has a round face and short ears. They are often confused with domestic mixed-breed cats but are purebred. Its breed name changed from domestic shorthair cats in 1966 to differentiate them. This medium-sized cat has a gentle nature that makes this breed a great companion for families with children or other pets. The American shorthair is one of the most popular pedigreed cat breeds in North America.
Breed Overview
Height: 8 to 10 inches
Weight: 8 to 12 pounds
Coat and Color: Shorthaired coat with a wide variety of possibilities, including white, black, cream, blue, brown, chinchilla, tortoiseshell, cameo, and many more
Life Expectancy: 15 to 20 years
Breeds to Avoid
If you are looking for one of the most popular breeds, you likely want a recognizable cat or a type you can find readily. You want to avoid scarce or rare breeds, like the Scottish fold or the Egyptian Mau. These cats are beautiful and make sweet housemates but are tough to find. Other cats you want to avoid are hybrid cats that carry on many wildcat traits, like chausies, toygers, and savannahs. They're often brilliant and athletic with a streak of unpredictability, but they're generally friendly around their human family.

<figcaption id="primary-image__figcap_1-0" class="comp primary-image__figcap mntl-figure-caption figure-article-caption"> <span class="figure-article-caption-owner">Credit: <p>The Spruce / Phoebe Cheong</p></span>
</figcaption></figure>
</div><div class="loc article-content"><div id="article-intro_1-0" class="comp article-intro mntl-block article-intro__content structured-content" data-contain-tooltips="true"><div id="article-intro__content_1-0" class="comp article-intro__content mntl-block"><div id="mntl-sc-block_1-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-callout--fastfacts lifestyle-sc-block-callout mntl-sc-block-callout mntl-block theme-fastfacts lifestyle-sc-block-callout--border" data-tracking-id="mntl-sc-block-callout" data-tracking-container="true"><h3 id="mntl-sc-block-callout-heading_1-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-callout-heading mntl-text-block">Key Takeaways</h3>
<div id="mntl-sc-block-callout-body_1-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-callout-body mntl-text-block"><ul><li>**Popularity and Characteristics:** The article highlights the distinctive characteristics and origins of the 12 most popular cat breeds, such as the Siamese, known for their intelligence and vocal skills, and the large and gentle Maine Coon.<br/></li><li>**Lifespan and Care:** It underscores the importance of indoor lifestyles for cats to ensure longevity and the need for grooming in breeds like Persians.<br/></li><li>**Pet Ownership Insights:** The piece provides insights on cat ownership, including which cat breeds are more suitable for families and those that might require extra care or attention.</li></ul></div></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_2-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-html mntl-sc-block-html"> These 12 cat breeds are the most popular worldwide. Whether due to their unique physical traits, appearances in movies, or affectionate personalities, they have left a lasting, positive impression on cat lovers globally.
</p>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_3-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-html mntl-sc-block-html"> A cat is either <a href="https://www.thesprucepets.com/what-is-a-domestic-cat-554691" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="1">domestic</a>, <a href="https://www.thesprucepets.com/what-breed-is-my-cat-554697" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="2">purebred</a>, or <a href="https://www.thesprucepets.com/hybrid-cat-breeds-4176576" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="3">hybrid</a>. Domestic cats lack a pedigree lineage, while purebreds have one. Hybrids result from crossbreeding domesticated cats with wild feline varieties. The United States-based Cat Fanciers' Association recognizes 45 distinct purebred cat breeds.
</p>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_4-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-html mntl-sc-block-html"> Read on to find out the 12 popular cat breeds around the world.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_5-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-callout--recipetip lifestyle-sc-block-callout mntl-sc-block-callout mntl-block theme-recipetip lifestyle-sc-block-callout--icon" data-tracking-id="mntl-sc-block-callout" data-tracking-container="true"><h3 id="mntl-sc-block-callout-heading_2-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-callout-heading mntl-text-block">Tip</h3>
<div id="mntl-sc-block-callout-body_2-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-callout-body mntl-text-block"><p>Indoor cats have the longest lifespans, living up to 20 years. Outdoor cats rarely make it past 10 years. Outdoor cats are prone to infections from other animals, can get into fights, may be attacked by predators, or may get run over by vehicles. Outdoor cats may also maim or kill outdoor birds and other wildlife.</p></div></div>

<p id="mntl-sc-block_9-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-html mntl-sc-block-html"> Popular since the 19th century, this cat breed originated in Thailand (formerly known as Siam). The Siamese has been a foundational breed for the Oriental shorthair, sphynx, and Himalayan. Most Siamese cats have distinct markings called "points," which are the areas of coloration on their face, ears, feet, and tail. Two varieties of Siamese cats have an "apple" shaped head and chubbier body and the other features a larger head and slender body. The animated Disney film "Lady and the Tramp" featured Siamese cats singing a song, demonstrating their intelligence and vocal skills. Many Siamese cats that originated from Thailand had a kink in their tail—a trait bred out of most Siamese, but you can still see it in street cats in Thailand.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_10-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-callout--breedffcat lifestyle-sc-block-callout mntl-sc-block-callout mntl-block theme-breedffcat lifestyle-sc-block-callout--breed" data-tracking-id="mntl-sc-block-callout" data-tracking-container="true"><h3 id="mntl-sc-block-callout-heading_3-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-callout-heading mntl-text-block">Breed Overview</h3>

</figcaption></figure>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_13-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-html mntl-sc-block-html"> Also lovingly referred to as one of the "smushed-face" cats, the Iranian cat, or Shiraz cat (named for a city in Iran), <a href="https://www.thesprucepets.com/persian-cats-gallery-4121944" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="1">Persians</a> have beautiful, long fur coats. They can come in almost any color and have a flat face when compared to most other breeds of cats. Persians are featured in many movies, artworks, and commercials and have been one of the most popular breeds of cats for decades. Their coats demand regular <a href="https://www.thesprucepets.com/best-cat-brushes-4590060" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="2">grooming</a>, otherwise, matting will result. Like many purebreds, they are prone to a variety of diseases, including renal and <a href="https://www.thesprucepets.com/heart-disease-in-cats-3384855" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="3">cardiac problems</a>.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_14-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-callout--breedffcat lifestyle-sc-block-callout mntl-sc-block-callout mntl-block theme-breedffcat lifestyle-sc-block-callout--breed" data-tracking-id="mntl-sc-block-callout" data-tracking-container="true"><h3 id="mntl-sc-block-callout-heading_4-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-callout-heading mntl-text-block">Breed Overview</h3>
<div id="mntl-sc-block-callout-body_4-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-callout-body mntl-text-block"><p><strong>Height:</strong> 14 to 18 inches</p><p><strong>Weight:</strong> 7 to 12 pounds</p><p><strong>Coat and Color:</strong> Long coat in solid (white, black, cream), tabby, calico, bi-color, silver and gold, shaded, and smoke</p><p><strong>Life Expectancy: </strong>15 to 20 years</p></div></div></div></div>
<div id="lifestyle-sc-block-adslot_2-0" class="comp lifestyle-sc-block-adslot mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block u-no-article-width-restriction"></div></li>

</figcaption></figure>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_17-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-html mntl-sc-block-html"> Known for its large stature and thick fur coat, the <a href="https://www.thesprucepets.com/maine-coon-cat-picture-gallery-4031806" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="1">Maine coon</a> is a cat that is difficult to ignore. Hailing from the state of Maine and the state’s official cat, the Maine coon is a gentle giant. They are great hunters and were popularized after the CFA recognized them as purebred in the late 1970s. They remain one of the most popular cat breeds. Many Maine coons have polydactylism, or extra toes, which makes their already large feet even larger.<span class="ql-inline-citation" data-cite="2"></span> This great feature helps for hunting in the snow since large feet act as snowshoes. The classic Maine coon coloration is a brown tabby, but this breed can come in almost any color.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_18-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-callout--breedffcat lifestyle-sc-block-callout mntl-sc-block-callout mntl-block theme-breedffcat lifestyle-sc-block-callout--breed" data-tracking-id="mntl-sc-block-callout" data-tracking-container="true"><h3 id="mntl-sc-block-callout-heading_5-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-callout-heading mntl-text-block">Breed Overview</h3>
<div id="mntl-sc-block-callout-body_5-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-callout-body mntl-text-block"><p><strong>Height:</strong> Up to 16 inches</p><p><strong>Weight:</strong> 8 to 20 pounds</p><p><strong>Coat and Color:</strong> Long, double coat and green, gold, or copper eyes</p><p><strong>Life Expectancy:</strong> 12 to 15 years</p></div></div></div></div>
<div id="lifestyle-sc-block-adslot_3-0" class="comp lifestyle-sc-block-adslot mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block u-no-article-width-restriction"></div></li>

<p id="mntl-sc-block_21-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-html mntl-sc-block-html"> <a href="https://www.thesprucepets.com/ragdoll-cats-gallery-4122006" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="1">Ragdoll cats</a> get their name from their docile temperament. They tend to go limp when picked up, much like a rag doll. At one time, people thought they couldn’t feel pain, but that is not true. Ragdolls look a lot like long-haired Siamese cats with pointed color patterns. They also have distinctive blue eyes and dog-like personalities, following their owners around the house.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_22-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-callout--breedffcat lifestyle-sc-block-callout mntl-sc-block-callout mntl-block theme-breedffcat lifestyle-sc-block-callout--breed" data-tracking-id="mntl-sc-block-callout" data-tracking-container="true"><h3 id="mntl-sc-block-callout-heading_6-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-callout-heading mntl-text-block">Breed Overview</h3>

<p id="mntl-sc-block_25-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-html mntl-sc-block-html"> Bengals are wild-looking cats—literally. Their markings make them look more like they belong in the jungle rather than in your home, but they are domesticated. They are talkative and require a lot of exercise. They come in many colors with patterns like spots and rosettes. Their name comes from the Asian leopard cat’s taxonomic name, <em>Prionailurus bengalensis. </em>Bengals were initially bred from domestic cats and a wildcat. They also get their beautiful patterns from this wildcat.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_26-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-callout--breedffcat lifestyle-sc-block-callout mntl-sc-block-callout mntl-block theme-breedffcat lifestyle-sc-block-callout--breed" data-tracking-id="mntl-sc-block-callout" data-tracking-container="true"><h3 id="mntl-sc-block-callout-heading_7-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-callout-heading mntl-text-block">Breed Overview</h3>
<div id="mntl-sc-block-callout-body_7-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-callout-body mntl-text-block"><p><strong>Height:</strong> 13 to 16 inches</p><p><strong>Weight:</strong> 6 to 12 pounds</p><p><strong>Coat and Color: </strong>Shorthaired, spotted, or marbled coat with patterns in black, chocolate, or gray/silver with green or gold eyes</p><p><strong>Life Expectancy: </strong>12 to 15 years</p></div></div></div></div>
<div id="lifestyle-sc-block-adslot_5-0" class="comp lifestyle-sc-block-adslot mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block u-no-article-width-restriction"></div></li>

<p id="mntl-sc-block_29-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-html mntl-sc-block-html"> Originally from Ethiopia (previously known as Abyssinia), the Abyssinian cat has a distinctive ruddy agouti coat that gives the breed its unique banded look. It has tall, pointy ears, a wedge-shaped head, and a slender and muscular body. Personality-wise, Abys are active, curious cats that frequently follow owners around. Their playful, dog-like demeanor endears them to their humans.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_30-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-callout--breedffcat lifestyle-sc-block-callout mntl-sc-block-callout mntl-block theme-breedffcat lifestyle-sc-block-callout--breed" data-tracking-id="mntl-sc-block-callout" data-tracking-container="true"><h3 id="mntl-sc-block-callout-heading_8-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-callout-heading mntl-text-block">Breed Overview</h3>
<div id="mntl-sc-block-callout-body_8-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-callout-body mntl-text-block"><p><strong>Height:</strong> 8 to 10 inches</p><p><strong>Weight:</strong> 8 to 10 pounds</p><p><strong>Coat and Color:</strong> Short-haired red/orange agouti fur coat with bands of light and dark coloring; other colors can include cinnamon, fawn, and blue</p><p><strong>Life Expectancy:</strong> 12 to 15 years</p></div></div></div></div>
<div id="lifestyle-sc-block-adslot_6-0" class="comp lifestyle-sc-block-adslot mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block u-no-article-width-restriction"></div></li>

<p id="mntl-sc-block_33-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-html mntl-sc-block-html"> Birmans are another color-pointed cat like the Siamese and ragdoll. They have blue eyes and a medium-long coat but no undercoat, the primary trait that sets them apart from Persians and Himalayans. Birmans were the original stock for breeding ragdolls, so they look very similar, but they have slightly different markings and personalities. They are fun, social cats that love attention—from their chosen person. They are more of a one-person cat than others.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_34-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-callout--breedffcat lifestyle-sc-block-callout mntl-sc-block-callout mntl-block theme-breedffcat lifestyle-sc-block-callout--breed" data-tracking-id="mntl-sc-block-callout" data-tracking-container="true"><h3 id="mntl-sc-block-callout-heading_9-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-callout-heading mntl-text-block">Breed Overview</h3>
<div id="mntl-sc-block-callout-body_9-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-callout-body mntl-text-block"><p><strong>Height:</strong> 8 to 10 inches</p><p><strong>Weight:</strong> 6 to 15 pounds</p><p><strong>Coat and Color:</strong> Long and silky coat in seal, blue, red, chocolate, cream, and tortie colors, including standard or lynx pattern points and deep blue eyes</p><p><strong>Life Expectancy:</strong> 15 to 20 years</p></div></div></div></div>
<div id="lifestyle-sc-block-adslot_7-0" class="comp lifestyle-sc-block-adslot mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block u-no-article-width-restriction"></div></li>

<p id="mntl-sc-block_37-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-html mntl-sc-block-html"> At a glance, you may think this slender cat looks like a Siamese, but the Oriental shorthair is a separate breed. Unlike Siamese cats, they usually have green eyes and many color patterns and colors. The Oriental can also have long hair. Oriental shorthair cats are prone to skin cancer and getting cold due to their lack of fur; they are often seen wearing sweaters.<span class="ql-inline-citation" data-cite="5"></span>
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_38-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-callout--breedffcat lifestyle-sc-block-callout mntl-sc-block-callout mntl-block theme-breedffcat lifestyle-sc-block-callout--breed" data-tracking-id="mntl-sc-block-callout" data-tracking-container="true"><h3 id="mntl-sc-block-callout-heading_10-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-callout-heading mntl-text-block">Breed Overview</h3>
<div id="mntl-sc-block-callout-body_10-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-callout-body mntl-text-block"><p><strong>Height:</strong> 9 to 11 inches</p><p><strong>Weight:</strong> 7 to 12 pounds</p><p><strong>Coat and Color:</strong> Short, smooth coat with many color possibilities: solids, shaded, smoke, and tabby with green, blue, gold, and yellow eyes</p><p><strong>Life Expectancy:</strong> 15 to 20 years</p></div></div></div></div>

<p id="mntl-sc-block_41-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-html mntl-sc-block-html"> If you are looking for a cat that doesn't shed, then the sphynx is for you. <a href="https://www.thesprucepets.com/cute-sphynx-cat-photos-4173584" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="1">Sphynx cats</a> look unique due to their lack of fur and are very social, active, dog-like felines. They look like they belong in the laps of royalty, but they are playful and goofy and act more like court jesters despite their regal appearance.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_42-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-callout--breedffcat lifestyle-sc-block-callout mntl-sc-block-callout mntl-block theme-breedffcat lifestyle-sc-block-callout--breed" data-tracking-id="mntl-sc-block-callout" data-tracking-container="true"><h3 id="mntl-sc-block-callout-heading_11-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-callout-heading mntl-text-block">Breed Overview</h3>
<div id="mntl-sc-block-callout-body_11-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-callout-body mntl-text-block"><p><strong>Height:</strong> 8 to 10 inches</p><p><strong>Weight:</strong> 8 to 10 pounds</p><p><strong>Coat and Color:</strong> Hairless with white, black, red, chocolate, lavender, tabby, tortoiseshell, calico, pointed, and mink colors</p><p><strong>Life Expectancy:</strong> 15 to 20 years</p></div></div></div></div>
<div id="lifestyle-sc-block-adslot_9-0" class="comp lifestyle-sc-block-adslot mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block u-no-article-width-restriction"></div></li>

<p id="mntl-sc-block_45-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-html mntl-sc-block-html"> Devon rex cats have wavy, soft short hair and slender bodies with enormous ears. The Devon Rex is a playful, active cat described as "a monkey in a catsuit." This kitty will steal your heart with its lively, sweet disposition. They are also trainable and adept at learning tricks.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_46-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-callout--breedffcat lifestyle-sc-block-callout mntl-sc-block-callout mntl-block theme-breedffcat lifestyle-sc-block-callout--breed" data-tracking-id="mntl-sc-block-callout" data-tracking-container="true"><h3 id="mntl-sc-block-callout-heading_12-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-callout-heading mntl-text-block">Breed Overview</h3>
<div id="mntl-sc-block-callout-body_12-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-callout-body mntl-text-block"><p><strong>Height:</strong> 10 to 12 inches</p><p><strong>Weight:</strong> 7 to 12 pounds</p><p><strong>Coat and Color:</strong> Short, fine, wavy coat in solid, tabby, and shaded varieties</p><p><strong>Life Expectancy:</strong> 15 to 20 years</p></div></div></div></div>
<div id="lifestyle-sc-block-adslot_10-0" class="comp lifestyle-sc-block-adslot mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block u-no-article-width-restriction"></div></li>

<span class="figure-article-caption-owner">Credit: <p>Kryssia Campos / Getty Images</p></span>
</figcaption></figure>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_49-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-html mntl-sc-block-html"> Like the Persian, the Himalayan cat is considered a sub-breed in some cat associations and a separate breed in others. Himalayans were bred from crossing Persians and Siamese to achieve their color points. They love attention and affection from their owners, have sweet personalities, and are playful.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_50-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-callout--breedffcat lifestyle-sc-block-callout mntl-sc-block-callout mntl-block theme-breedffcat lifestyle-sc-block-callout--breed" data-tracking-id="mntl-sc-block-callout" data-tracking-container="true"><h3 id="mntl-sc-block-callout-heading_13-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-callout-heading mntl-text-block">Breed Overview</h3>
<div id="mntl-sc-block-callout-body_13-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-callout-body mntl-text-block"><p><strong>Height:</strong> 10 to 12 inches</p><p><strong>Weight:</strong> 9 to 12 pounds</p><p><strong>Coat and Color:</strong>  Dense undercoat with a long overcoat that comes in pointed colors and patterns in gray, blue, chocolate, lilac, and cream</p><p><strong>Life Expectancy:</strong> Up to 18 years</p></div></div></div></div>
<div id="lifestyle-sc-block-adslot_11-0" class="comp lifestyle-sc-block-adslot mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block u-no-article-width-restriction"></div></li>
<li id="sc-list__item_12-0" class="comp sc-list__item li mntl-block"><div id="list-sc-item_12-0" class="comp list-sc-item div mntl-block"><div id="mntl-list-marker_12-0" class="comp mntl-list-marker mntl-list-marker--numbers"> <div class="content-list-number">

</div>
</div>
<div id="sc-list-item_12-0" class="comp sc-list-item mntl-sc-list-item mntl-sc-page mntl-block" data-list-index="12"><span class="heading-toc" id="toc-american-shorthair"></span><h2 id="mntl-sc-block_51-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-heading mntl-sc-block-heading lifestyle-headings mntl-sc-list-item-title"> <a href="https://www.thesprucepets.com/american-shorthair-cat-breed-profile-4774399" rel="nocaes" class="mntl-sc-block-heading__link" >American Shorthair</a> </h2>

<figcaption id="mntl-figure-caption_12-0" class="comp mntl-figure-caption text-utility-100 figure-article-caption"> <span class="figure-article-caption-text">American shorthair cats can come in many patterns and colors.</span>
<span class="figure-article-caption-owner">Credit: <p>Marc Henrie / Getty Images</p></span>
</figcaption></figure>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_53-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-html mntl-sc-block-html"> An all-American breed, the American shorthair cat is an intelligent, shorthaired feline that has a round face and short ears. They are often confused with domestic mixed-breed cats but are purebred. Its breed name changed from domestic shorthair cats in 1966 to differentiate them.<span class="ql-inline-citation" data-cite="4"></span> This medium-sized cat has a gentle nature that makes this breed a great companion for families with children or other pets. The American shorthair is one of the most popular pedigreed cat breeds in North America.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_54-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-callout--breedffcat lifestyle-sc-block-callout mntl-sc-block-callout mntl-block theme-breedffcat lifestyle-sc-block-callout--breed" data-tracking-id="mntl-sc-block-callout" data-tracking-container="true"><h3 id="mntl-sc-block-callout-heading_14-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-callout-heading mntl-text-block">Breed Overview</h3>
<div id="mntl-sc-block-callout-body_14-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-callout-body mntl-text-block"><p><strong>Height: </strong>8 to 10 inches</p><p><strong>Weight:</strong> 8 to 12 pounds</p><p><strong>Coat and Color:</strong> Shorthaired coat with a wide variety of possibilities, including white, black, cream, blue, brown, chinchilla, tortoiseshell, cameo, and many more</p><p><strong>Life Expectancy:</strong> 15 to 20 years</p></div></div></div></div>
<div id="lifestyle-sc-block-adslot_12-0" class="comp lifestyle-sc-block-adslot mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block u-no-article-width-restriction"></div></li></ul>
<div id="article-outro_1-0" class="comp article-outro mntl-block structured-content sc-list__content"><span class="heading-toc" id="toc-breeds-to-avoid"></span><h2 id="mntl-sc-block_55-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-heading mntl-sc-block-heading lifestyle-headings"> <span class="mntl-sc-block-heading__text text-title-300"> Breeds to Avoid </span> </h2>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_56-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block lifestyle-sc-block-html mntl-sc-block-html"> If you are looking for one of the most popular breeds, you likely want a recognizable cat or a type you can find readily. You want to avoid <a href="https://www.thesprucepets.com/rarest-cat-breeds-4797965" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="1">scarce or rare breeds</a>, like the <a href="https://www.thesprucepets.com/scottish-fold-profile-551898" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="2">Scottish fold</a> or the <a href="https://www.thesprucepets.com/egyptian-mau-cat-breed-profile-5323874" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="3">Egyptian Mau</a>. These cats are beautiful and make sweet housemates but are tough to find. Other cats you want to avoid are hybrid cats that carry on many wildcat traits, like <a href="https://www.thesprucepets.com/chausie-cat-breed-profile-5188031" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="4">chausies</a>, <a href="https://www.thesprucepets.com/toyger-cat-profile-554213" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="5">toygers</a>, and <a href="https://www.thesprucepets.com/savannah-breed-profile-554212" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="6">savannahs</a>. They're often brilliant and athletic with a streak of unpredictability, but they're generally friendly around their human family.
</p>
